patches.summary: bulk: refuse sleep/turbo/auto on non-fan devices instead of sending a no-op windmode/windtype frame.
patches.reason: windmode/windtype are fan firmware fields; heaters, purifiers, humidifiers, and ACs accept the WebSocket frame but ignore it, so the command reported success while doing nothing. partitionForAction now filters non-fans for fan-only actions and errors out when no fan-applicable devices remain.

patches.summary: rooms: convert per-device temperatures to °C using tempunit before averaging, and rename the output column to AVG_TEMP_C / avg_temperature_c.
patches.reason: Each Dreo device carries its own tempunit (0=°C, 1=°F). Summing raw temperature across mixed-unit households produced meaningless averages (e.g. 22 + 72 → 47). The accumulator now reads tempunit per device, converts °F → °C, and the column header/JSON key now names the unit so consumers can't misread it.

patches.summary: sensors: normalize per-device temperatures to °C using tempunit and rename the output column to TEMP_C / temperature_c so the sibling sensors and rooms commands display on the same scale.
patches.reason: rooms.go was fixed in the prior patch but the whole-house sensors snapshot still showed raw temperature values — a device reporting 72 °F and one reporting 22 °C appeared in the same TEMP column with no unit annotation. Same tempunit guard (convert when tempunit==1) and same column-naming convention now apply, keeping the two commands consistent.

patches.summary: auth login: replace the post-login "Token saved to <path>" stdout line with one that names the credential persistence and the reason (Dreo issues no refresh_token, so user/pass is kept on disk to re-mint the bearer).
patches.reason: The PR description previously claimed the plaintext password was never persisted to disk, but config.go intentionally serializes DreoPassword to the 0600 config file so unattended re-login can mint a fresh bearer (Dreo's OAuth response carries no refresh_token). The user-facing success line now reflects what actually happens; README.md already documents this accurately.
